operating_state: Total revenues increased 58% to $57.14 billion for nine months ended September 30, 2022, driven by automotive sales growth from increased Model 3/Y deliveries and higher average selling prices from increased Model Y proportion. Production ramping at Shanghai and Fremont facilities, combined with new production at Berlin-Brandenburg and Texas Gigafactories, supported record quarterly vehicle deliveries. Energy generation and storage revenue grew from increased Powerwall and Megapack deployments, though automotive remains the primary growth driver with strength across international markets.
cost_structure: Total automotive gross margin improved to 29.6% for nine months from 28.6% prior year, despite rising raw material, commodity, logistics and expedite costs including idle capacity charges from temporary Shanghai shutdown and Texas facility ramping. Manufacturing costs remained elevated due to supply chain constraints, semiconductor shortages, and raw material price inflation affecting lithium, nickel, and cobalt inputs for battery production, partially offset by production scaling efficiencies.
material_events: Gigafactory Berlin-Brandenburg and Texas commenced production in 2022, with Texas beginning Model Y deliveries using Tesla-made 4680 cells and structural battery packs. Tesla Semi entered early production at Nevada facility with customer deliveries planned for December 2022. Board declared three-for-one stock split effective as stock dividend and appointed Joseph Gebbia as director. Company recorded bitcoin impairment losses and gains from conversions.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 impacts persisted through Shanghai lockdowns causing temporary facility shutdowns and supply chain disruptions. Industry-wide semiconductor shortages continued requiring manufacturing workarounds and expedited delivery costs, while inflationary pressures from supply chain disruptions, increased shipping costs, and raw material inflation contributed to product pricing adjustments. Operations face exposure to geopolitical tensions from Russia-Ukraine conflict affecting supply chains and currency exchange rate fluctuations.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures of $6.00 to $8.00 billion in 2022 and each of the next two fiscal years to support global production expansion and new battery cell manufacturing capabilities. Company committed to Tesla Semi customer deliveries beginning December 2022 and continues development of Cybertruck, Roadster, and Robotaxi products. Strategic focus remains on increasing vehicle production capacity, improving FSD capabilities, expanding global infrastructure, and ramping energy storage manufacturing.
uncertainty_profile: Production ramp success at new facilities depends on resolving semiconductor and component constraints, maintaining stable workforce amid labor shortages, and executing technological targets for battery cells and manufacturing improvements. Government investigations remain ongoing with potential for penalties, while electric vehicle incentive programs face reduction risks. Digital asset holdings remain subject to impairment charges from market volatility, and convertible debt conversions could dilute shareholders while CEO stock pledges create potential forced selling pressure.
